title: adiantum capillus-veneris subsp. trifidum
intro: Adiantum capillus-veneris subsp. trifidum, also known as the Venus hair fern or Maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Pteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Adiantum capillus-veneris subsp. trifidum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceutical biology", adiantum capillus-veneris subsp. trifidum may be advocated as a potential antiobesity agent due to its significant antiobesity and triacylglycerol-reducing capacities.
